new_exact:
IV

[¶6] The judgment is affirmed in part. J.D.'s appeal is dismissed.

[¶7] Jon J. Jensen, C.J. Daniel J. Crothers Lisa Fair McEvers Jerod E. Tufte Douglas A. Bahr
evidence: The source PDF shows section heading IV immediately before [¶6] (with one blank line between them). The DB has incorrectly placed IV between [¶6] and [¶7]. This is a paragraph sequencing error during OCR/ingestion: the Roman numeral section heading belongs at the start of the section, not in the middle of it. Confirmed by comparing PDF layout structure where IV clearly precedes the [¶6] paragraph.

note: Paragraph marker and comma improperly split across lines (3 instances).
Paragraph marker and comma improperly split across lines (2 instances).

old_exact: the performance inquiry must be whether the circumstances.'" Id. at 478. Instead of a per se rule, the Court used a circumstance specific analysis. Id. counsel's assistance was considering all reasonable
new_exact: the performance inquiry must be whether counsel's assistance was reasonable considering all the circumstances.'" Id. at 478. Instead of a per se rule, the Court used a circumstance specific analysis. Id.
evidence: The DB text shows a truncated/corrupted quote in paragraph [¶7] reading 'the performance inquiry must be whether the circumstances.' followed by orphaned/garbled text ('counsel's assistance was considering all reasonable'). The PDF shows the complete, correct quote: 'the performance inquiry must be whether counsel's assistance was reasonable considering all the circumstances.' This is part of the discussion of Strickland's holding in the Roe v. Flores-Ortega analysis. The error represents missing middle text ('counsel's assistance was reasonable considering all the ') from a broken sentence.
note: High-risk content correction: legal citation/quote text. The quoted material is substantive law from Strickland v. Washington and is essential to the opinion's reasoning. The DB version is clearly corrupted with text truncation and orphaned fragments. The PDF version shows the authoritative source text reconstructed from court's official filing.

old_exact: and the individual's diagnosed disorder.  Matter of J.M., 2019 ND 125,
new_exact: and the individual's diagnosed disorder. Matter of J.M., 2019 ND 125,
evidence: PDF source shows single regular space (U+0020) after 'disorder.' followed by 'Matter'. DB text contains a non-breaking space (U+202F) followed by a regular space, which is an ingestion artifact. The non-breaking space should be removed to match the authoritative PDF.
note: Located in paragraph 5, between the citation ending 'diagnosed disorder.' and the next citation 'Matter of J.M.'

old_exact: accord fact. The to other administrative agencies' Commission's decisions
new_exact: accord to other administrative agencies' findings of fact. The Commission's decisions
evidence: PDF source (pdftotext extraction, lines 112-114) shows 'accord\n      to other administrative agencies' findings of fact. The\n      Commission's decisions' which flowed to a continuous sentence in the DB. The DB version is missing 'to' after 'accord' and is missing 'findings of fact' entirely, instead incorrectly saying 'accord fact. The to other administrative agencies'.
note: This is a critical OCR/text extraction error in [¶7] that disrupts the sentence flow. The phrase 'than we ordinarily accord to other administrative agencies' findings of fact' is the correct statutory standard language.

old_exact: set forth by the findings of legislature
evidence:
PDF source (pdftotext extraction, lines 118-121) shows the phrase spanning a page break: 'set forth by the

                                      2
\x0c      legislature' (where \x0c is the form-feed byte between pages). The correct text is 'statutory standards set forth by the legislature.' The DB incorrectly includes 'findings of' before 'legislature'.
note: In [¶7], the phrase should be 'the statutory standards set forth by the legislature' not 'the findings of legislature'. The extra 'findings of' appears to be a text extraction artifact.

evidence: Citation 2009 ND 9, ¶ 5 in City of Fargo v. Lunday: erroneous line breaks split comma from reporter citation number; verified in source PDF
Citation 2019 ND 239, ¶ 15 in State v. Smith: erroneous line breaks split comma from reporter citation number; verified in source PDF

source: PDF line break at end of line 46: 'problem-' continues as 'solving' on line 47
evidence: The authoritative PDF shows standard hyphenation with 'problem-' at line end and 'solving' continuing on the next line. The DB has incorrectly rendered this as 'problem- solving' with a space after the hyphen, creating a false word split. Normalized comparison confirms all other text content matches PDF exactly.

source: Official PDF caption - judge listed as 'the Honorable Benjamen J. Johnson, Judge'
evidence: Verified by visual inspection of source PDF page 1; database has misspelled the judge's first name
PDF clearly shows 'H.R.B.' with capital R, not L. OCR engine misread the letter R as L in the child's initials. This is the first of three instances of this same OCR error.
Same OCR confusion of R and L in child's initial. PDF clearly shows H.R.B., which is the correct child designation throughout the opinion.

old_exact: that changing H.L.B.'s name would not
new_exact: that changing H.R.B.'s name would not
evidence: Third and final instance of R/L OCR confusion. PDF confirms H.R.B. is the correct child designation. All three H.L.B. instances in paragraphs 21-23 are OCR errors that should be H.R.B.

source: PDF pages 1-2: page break artifact where 'was killed' is split across pages; OCR/ingestion captured both instances of 'was'

[page 1 footer]
[page 2 header]
was killed' — pdftotext reconstructs this with the page break (0x0c byte) and page header visible. The DB ingestion process incorrectly included both 'was' instances.
